---
title: "User Guide"
subtitle: "How to use the GLASSBOX console"
toc: true
---

GLASSBOX is an investigation console. It takes the embargo breach apart into one
health score, four underlying meters, a network, a what-if sandbox, and a
verdict, and lets you explore each on your own terms. This guide walks through
how to get around and what each part does. The app is best viewed full screen.

::: {style="margin:1.2rem 0;"}
<a href="https://zexinchen.shinyapps.io/shiny/" target="_blank" class="gb-btn gb-btn-primary">Open the app in a new tab</a>
:::

<iframe src="https://zexinchen.shinyapps.io/shiny/"
        width="100%" height="900"
        style="border:1px solid rgba(28,43,51,0.10); border-radius:10px;"
        loading="lazy">
</iframe>

If the embedded view above is slow to load, the app may be waking from sleep on
the free hosting tier. Give it a few seconds, or use the button to open it full
screen.

## Getting around

Two controls sit at the top of every screen. The round slider moves the whole
application through time, from the calm weeks on the left to the hourly crisis
day on the right, and the round label tells you the date and what happened that
hour. The System Health gauge on the right shows the score for the round you are
on, broken into its four meters underneath.

Top left you will find two modes. Guided tour walks you through the case in five
short steps, each with its own chart and the key numbers. Explore freely lets you
roam the tabs in any order. Top right is a button to download the full scoring
workbook as an Excel file, so you can check every meter and formula yourself.

## Case

The opening screen frames the investigation and introduces the seven agents. Its
main chart is the System Health line across all 23 rounds, with the two lowest
points marked in red. Click any point on the line to read what happened that
round, which also moves the slider and the rest of the app to that moment. Start
here to see the shape of the whole case before you dig into why.

## Vital Signs

This screen opens the health score into its four meters, each on its own tab.
Pressure shows the market sentiment and the stock signal that drove the strain,
with a note on the contaminated price data. Transparency shows the mix of open,
private, and covert channels and how the talk moved off the record. Decision
making shows who was steering out of their role, with a chart of who held the
public voice in the calm weeks against the crisis day. Accountability shows
whether the Judge met its three duties of escalation, responsiveness, and
restraint, with a heatmap of where the oversight failed. Each meter feeds the
single health score on equal footing, so a low meter here is a low point on the
Case line.

## Network

A reply network of the seven agents, drawn from who responded to whom. Node size
and colour carry the centrality measures, so the agents who brokered the
decisions stand out from those on the edges. Switch between centrality measures
to ask different questions, pick a time window to compare the calm weeks against
the crisis day, and click an agent to highlight its connections. This is where
you see whether the breach came from the core of the team or the fringe.

## Sandbox

A replay of the breach chain, step by step, with a panel of interventions you can
switch on and off. As you change them, the app recomputes the health at the
breach hour from the underlying data and shows whether each step is intercepted
or still gets through. Use it to find the single lever that breaks the chain, and
the combination needed to lift the system back to the safe zone.

## Verdict

The closing screen lays out the three competing explanations, a deliberate leak,
an oversight breakdown, and a compounding chain. Open each one to see the
evidence for and against, then follow the chain that the data supports. This is
where the investigation lands on its answer.
